title: Dianthus laevigatus
taxonomy: Dianthus laevigatus, also known as smooth pink or pink maidens, belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Caryophyllaceae family. The Caryophyllaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pink or carnation family. Dianthus laevigatus is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. Within the Caryophyllaceae family, Dianthus laevigatus is part of the genus Dianthus, which includes a variety of flowering plants known as pinks.
uses: Dianthus laevigatus, also known as the smooth pink or pink flowered pink, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
